learning_outcomes:
1. Major ProgrammeUpon
completing the programme, students will be able to: Knowledge
domain1. Acquire
the contemporary concepts of public health and the interactions between
determinants of health at individual, local and global levels.2. Explain
from global perspective the burden of disease, the links between health and
development, and approaches to international cooperation to monitor, promote
and protect health.3. Describe
the important issues involved in public health advocacy, including ethical
reasoning, civic engagement, and social justice. Skill
domain1. Assesse
the health status, social concern, needs and equity of different populations
contributing to the use of health services and health promotion practices.2. Design
framework for primary, secondary and tertiary care and prevention. 3. Utilize
evidence based approach to inquire, analyze and criticize contemporary public
health issues. Value
domain1. Demonstrate
the appreciation of the importance of primary care in population health.2. Acknowledge
the cross-cultural impacts on actual and perceived health needs.3. Uphold
the core value of social equity and justice.
study_scheme:
Public Health Public Health Applicable to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 68 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  10
MEDF1011, 1021, 1031, 1041, PHPC1012
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  32
PHPC1017, 1022, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 3012, 3027, 3028
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  22
22 units of courses chosen from the
following, with 15 units of courses chosen from Group A and 7 units of
courses chosen from Group B:
Group A (with a maximum of 6
units chosen from courses labeled as #): ANTH4330#, CHPR4011, 4012,
GPAD3255#, GRON3412#, PHPC2007, 2020, 3010, 3014[a], 3015, 3016, 3017, 3018, 3024, 3026, 3034, 3415, 4025, 4028, 4029, 4031, 4032, 4033, 4040, 4041, 4042,
4043, SOCI3227#, SPED2540#, 4560#, 4570# Group B: One combination[b] of courses chosen
from the following: i) PHPC4911[c], 4912[c] and 4913[c] ii) PHPC3014[a], 4921[c] and 4922[c]
4.  |  Experiential
Learning:  |  4
PHPC1019, 1020, 4930[c]
[d]
Total:  |  68
Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  PHPC and CHPR courses at 2000 and above level as well
as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA
for honours classification.
[a]  |  The course taken to fulfill the requirements
specified in Elective Courses (Group B) cannot be counted towards the
fulfilment of the requirements specified in Elective Courses (Group A).
[b]  |  Students are required to seek approval from the Major
Programme on the choice of the combination of elective courses.
[c]  |  Capstone
courses
[d]  |  Students are required to complete 100 hours of
fieldwork.

Minor Programme Title Public Health
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 18 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  6
PHPC1001, 1017, 2017
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  12
12 units of courses chosen from the
following, with at least 6 units of courses at 3000 or above level: PHPC1022, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2015,
2016, 2020, 3012, 3014, 3016, 3017, 3024, 3028, 4028, 4029, 4031, 4033
Total:  |  18
Explanatory Note:
1.  |  This Minor Programme is not applicable to students
who major in Community Health Practice, Gerontology, and Biotechnology,
Entrepreneurship and Healthcare Management.
